steps.action: The Lezginka (Russian: Лезгинка) is a folk dance with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: The Lezginka (Russian: Лезгинка) is a folk dance with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: It uses a fast 68 rhythm, and can be either a so
steps.text: It uses a fast 68 rhythm, and can be either a solo male or a pair dance.
According to Encyclopædia Britannica: Lezginka, also spelled Lezghinka, folk dance originating among the Lezgin people of the North Caucasus.

steps.action: It is a male solo dance (often with a sword) and
steps.text: It is a male solo dance (often with a sword) and also a couple dance.

steps.action: The man, imitating the eagle, falls to his knees
steps.text: The man, imitating the eagle, falls to his knees, leaps up, and dances with concise steps and strong, sharp arm and body movements.

steps.action: When the dance is performed in pairs, couples do
steps.text: When the dance is performed in pairs, couples do not touch; the woman dances quietly as she regards the man’s display.

steps.action: The dance has also been adapted into classical m
steps.text: The dance has also been adapted into classical music in Aram Khachaturian's 1942 ballet Gayane.
